name: Bouillabaisse (Fish Stew)
recipeIngredient: 2 Cans whole peeled tomatoes, undrained (1 lb. 12 oz.)
recipeInstructions:
1. Heat olive oil in an 8-quart kettle.
2. Add chopped onion and 3 cloves garlic; sauté until the onion is tender, about 5 minutes.
3. Add clam juice, undrained tomatoes, 3/4 cup water, orange peel spiral, salt, saffron, fennel seed, thyme, pepper, and bay leaves; bring to a boil.
4. Reduce heat and simmer mixture, covered for 30 minutes, stirring occasionally.
5. Remove from heat and let cool, about 1 hour. Then refrigerate, covered, overnight.
6. The next day, with a sharp knife, cut the frozen lobster tails (shell and all) in half crosswise.
7. Wipe cod and halibut with damp paper towels.
8. Cut into 1 1/2-inch pieces or leave whole.
9. Rinse sea scallops in running cold water; drain.
10. Bring tomato mixture to a boil. Add frozen lobster. Simmer, covered for 4 minutes.
11. Add cod and halibut pieces; simmer covered for 10 minutes.
12. Add sea scallops over low heat, 5 minutes.
13. Spoon bouillabaisse over garlic toast. You can substitute different varieties of fish, if desired.
